title: A method for bringing down the number of bookkeeping notes when paying back a financial loan with interests.
abstract: The present invention concerns a method for bringing down the number of bookkeeping notes when paying back a financial loan with interests by a non-profit organization (10) in a fundraising program managed by an intermediary (11), said funds being given by a plurality of supporters (13). According to the invention, the aggregated sum (S) given by the plurality of supporters and received by said non-profit organization, is returned as a whole by the same non-profit organization to said intermediary with an interest rate which is the arithmetic mean of the interest rates selected by each supporter for his given sum of money, and it is returned by said intermediary to each supporter after said aggregated sum has been broken down into the respective sums of money given by each supporter of the plurality of supporters, including the interest calculated with the rate selected by the respective supporter.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for bringing down the number of bookkeeping notes when paying back a financial loan with interests by a non-profit organization (10) in a fundraising program managed by an intermediary (11) said funds being given by a plurality of supporters (13), said method comprising the steps of: - storing one or more socially-oriented projects (14a, 14b, 14c) in a first server (6, 7) which is in signal communication with a computer network (5); - providing a communication interface (9) which is in signal communication with said first server (6, 7) through said computer network (5), said interface (9) being configured to interact by means of one or more electronic devices (2, 3, 4) of each of said supporters (13), - selecting, through said communication interface (9), at least one of said socially-oriented projects (14a, 14b, 14c) proposed by a first non-profit organization (10), - receiving by said non-profit organization on its given account open at said intermediary one or more sums of money (18b) given by each of said supporters (13) for said selected socially- oriented project (14a, 14b, 14c), through said communication interface (9, generating an aggregated sum of money); - each supporter selecting an interest rate for his given sum of money ranging from zero to a maximum value, through said communication interface (9); - returning the sum of money given by each supporter with said interest rate to each respective supporter within a first predetermined period of time (Tl), wherein: - the aggregated sum (S) given by the plurality of supporters and received by said non-profit organization is returned as a whole by the same non-profit organization to said intermediary with an interest rate which is the arithmetic mean of the interest rates selected by each supporter for his given sum of money, and it is returned by said intermediary to each supporter after said aggregated sum has been broken down into the respective sums of money given by each supporter of the plurality of supporters, including the interest calculated with the rate selected by the respective 

 supporter. 2. A method for bringing down the number of bookkeeping notes when paying back a financial loan with interest by a non-profit organization according to claim 1, wherein the advance withholding taxes which are due on the loan interests to be received by each lender, are calculated and managed by the intermediary in the name and on behalf of each lender and charged as a whole on the account of the non-profit organization. 3. A method as claimed in claim 1, comprising the steps of - checking whether the aggregate amount (S) of said sum of money is equal to or greater than a predetermined threshold value (Vth), and - checking whether this predetermined threshold value (Vth) has been reached within a second predetermined period of time (T2). 4. A method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the value of said interest rate to be selected by each supporter is a function of the length of said predetermined period of time (Tl) indicated by the non-profit organization (10) that proposes the project. 5. A method as claimed in claim 1, wherein if said aggregate amount of money (S) is equal to or greater than said predetermined threshold value (Vth) and if the threshold value (Vth) has been reached within the period of time (T2), then said method comprises the additional step of sending a first signal (SI) to each of said plurality of supporters (13) and/or said non-profit organization (10) and/or said intermediary (11) to inform that said threshold value (Vth) has been reached within said period of time (T2). 6. A method as claimed in claim 1, wherein if said aggregate amount of money (S) is lower than said predetermined threshold value (Vth) by a value (Î,Î'), preferably not exceding 30% of said predetermined threshold value (Vth) and/or if such predetermined threshold value (Vth) has been reached within said period of time (T2), then said method comprises the additional step of sending a second signal (S2, S2') to each of the supporters (13) and/or said non-profit organization (10) and/or said intermediary (11) to inform that said threshold value (Vth) has not been reached within said period of time (T2). 7. A method as claimed in claim 1, comprising the additional step of creating at least one account to be associated with one or more of said socially-oriented projects (14a, 14b, 14c), said at least one account being stored in said first server (6, 7). 8. A method as claimed in claim 5, comprising the additional step of transferring said aggregate amount of money (S) through said computer network (5) to said at least one account associated with said selected socially-oriented project (14a, 14b, 14c). 9. A method as claimed in claim 1, wherein, once said selected socially-oriented project (14a, 14b, 14c) has been completed, said method comprises the step of crediting said aggregate amount (S) to another account, which can be associated with the next non-profit organization in a plurality "n" of additional non-profit organizations, next to said first non-profit organization (10). 10. A method as claimed in claim 9, wherein, if said aggregate amount (S) has not been transferred to said additional account associated to said next non-profit organization, then said method comprises the additional steps of: - sending a third signal (S3) to each supporter of said plurality of supporters (13) to indicate that said money transfer did not take place; - changing a reputation coefficient of said non-profit organization which failed to receive said aggregate amount (S). 11. A method as claimed in claim 4, wherein said predetermined threshold value (Vth) is determined according to the economic value of the selected socially-oriented project. 12. A method as claimed in claim 4, wherein said interval of a second predetermined period of time (T2) is two months or less. 13. A method as claimed in claim 1, comprising the additional step of: - providing personal data (21) of said supporters (13); - submitting a second data processing form (22) to said supporters (13), said form authorizing handling of said personal data (21) of said supporters (13); - storing said personal data (21) in said first server (6). 14. A method as claimed in claim 1, comprising the additional step of displaying a merit 

 index in said communication interface (9), said merit index being calculated according to operating parameters of the non-profit organization (10). 15. An IT product designed to be directly loaded into the memory of a computer system, comprising program code portions, and adapted to carry out the method as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 14, when it runs on said computer system. 16. A system of raising funds for a non-profit organization (10) by means of a fundraising program managed by an intermediary (11), said funds (18b) being given by at least one supporter (13), said system comprising: - a communication interface (9) in signal communication with a first server (6, 7) through a computer network (5), said interface (9) being configured to interact by means of one or more electronic devices (2, 3, 4) of said at least one supporter (13); - said first server (6) comprising first storage means (8), which are adapted to store information concerning one or more socially-oriented projects (14a, 14b, 14c), managed by at least one non-profit organization (10) and at least one processor; characterized in that said first server (6) is configured to implement said IT product as claimed in claim 15.
